area: Kernel-wide glue (ordering, evidence deadlines, parameter surface)
sources: docs/core/DECISIONS.md entry 49 da-windows S5 (lines 1067-1068, provisional pending DK lines 1043-1047)
docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md Item 5 B2 gate (line 141, ChatLunatique signs CONSENSUS_PARAMS surface)

inputs.attackFlagText: A kernel API that accepts (K, W, C) but validates them against compile-time defaults (or falls back to defaults when absent) is a baked constant wearing a parameter signature — the two-parameterization vectors (G11) are the only mechanical detector.
inputs.predicateSignature: daKernelConstruct(K, W, C) -> {verdict}; daVerdict parameterized by (K,W,C) inputs with no constant in @ont/consensus

fixture.caseHardcodedDefaultsFailsSecondParamete: a reference kernel with K/W/C hardcoded (or defaulting when a param is absent) passes the (6,2,3) DA suite but FAILS at a second valid parameterization (e.g. (10,3,4)) -> the baked-default kernel is non-conformant -> REJECT
fixture.caseNoDefaultConstructionRejects: a construction that falls back to a default when a parameter is absent must instead reject (no silent fallback)
fixture.caseTrueParametricPasses: a true parametric kernel passes at both (6,2,3) and (10,3,4) — the companion that the requirement is satisfiable
fixture.params: #49 S5/S7: (K,W,C) are per-network inputs, no constant in @ont/consensus; the mechanical detector is the two-parameterization vector (G11)

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on da-windows (#49): the verdict (a kernel validating (K,W,C) against compile-time defaults, or falling back to defaults, is a baked constant wearing a parameter signature and must fail) is derivable from #49 §6e S5 ('(K,W,C) are per-network consensus parameters, passed to the kernel as inputs, no constant in @ont/consensus') plus the ratified B2 CONSENSUS_PARAMS gate (SOFTWARE_CANON Item 5), but §6e/#49 is PROVISIONAL pending DK. This is the G-area parametric-kernel / no-baked-constant instance of the D12/Z13 S5/S7 family; the mechanical detector (two-parameterization vectors) is G11. Depends on #49 ⇒ provisional-vector.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored from a provisional vector (decisionDeps + flipMarker shed on ratification); status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); re-targets on DK's #49 parameter-set ruling.

expected.invariant: (K,W,C) are kernel inputs with no constant in @ont/consensus (#49 S5); a kernel that validates them against compile-time defaults or falls back to defaults is a baked constant and is rejected — proven by failing at a second valid parameterization while passing the first; identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: The verdict — a kernel that accepts (K,W,C) but validates against compile-time defaults or falls back to defaults is a baked constant wearing a parameter signature, and must fail — is derivable from da-windows §6e S5 ('(K,W,C) are per-network consensus parameters, passed to the kernel as inputs, no constant in @ont/consensus') and the ratified B2 gate ('ChatLunatique signs the CONSENSUS_PARAMS surface'). But §6e/#49 is PROVISIONAL pending DK ratification, so the parametric-kernel requirement rests on a provisional decision. The detector itself (two-parameterization vectors) is G11.

area: Kernel-wide glue (ordering, evidence deadlines, parameter surface)
sources: docs/core/SOFTWARE_CANON.md L2 ownership-kernel row (line 29, 'No DB, no network, no clock') and boundary rule (lines 34-42)
docs/core/B2_KERNEL_HARDENING.md G6 (line 2305, step-3 restatement line 2295, blocked-on recovery-auth line 2297)

inputs.attackFlagText: Evaluation-time availability (the legacy callback) makes two honest replayers disagree about whether a RecoverOwner ever created pendingRecovery — a per-name ownership fork, the §3 hazard at the recovery layer.
inputs.predicateSignature: recoveryPendingCreate(witnessedDescriptorChainEvidence, witnessedChainFacts, priorState) -> {verdict}  // pure, no evaluation-time availability callback

fixture.caseEvalTimeCallbackForks: two evaluators with identical chain + identical witnessed descriptor-chain evidence but DIFFERENT local receipt timelines; an implementation whose pendingRecovery-creation verdict differs because it consulted an evaluation-time availability callback is non-conformant -> REJECT (pins kernel I/O-purity for recovery; fail-closed on the I/O hole)
fixture.casePureConvergence: the conformant kernel ranges only over the witnessed descriptor-chain evidence (no I/O, no clock) so both evaluators converge on the same pendingRecovery verdict — the companion accept
fixture.priorState: an armed descriptor; a RecoverOwner invoke whose pendingRecovery creation an availability callback would make receipt-relative

inputs.scopeNote: PROVISIONAL on BOTH recovery-auth (#50) AND da-windows (#49) — dual-dep. The CORE verdict (an evaluation-time availability callback making pendingRecovery creation receipt-relative is a conformance failure; the kernel must be a pure deterministic predicate — no I/O, no clock — fail-closed on the I/O hole) is derivable NOW from ratified SOFTWARE_CANON ('No DB, no network, no clock' + the L2 boundary rule). But applying it to RecoverOwner pendingRecovery depends on #50 (provisional) to fix WHICH evidence the kernel evaluates (descriptor chain only under b1) and on #49 for the §6e clock the predicate reads. The purity verdict STANDS under both flips; only the evidence object (#50) and the h_r clock (#49) re-instantiate. Depends on #49+#50 ⇒ provisional-vector. Authored as one vector (not split) because the verdict is invariant under both rulings — only its input fixtures shift.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: provisional vector with decisionDeps (dual: #50 + #49) + flipMarker; status locked (per-area adversarial review passed; authorityTier RATIFIED 2026-06-14 (DK ratified da-windows #49 O1 / recovery-auth #50 b1); lineage in DECISIONS #49/#50, registry Part B, decision papers, git); the purity verdict holds under any #49/#50 combination, only the evidence-object/clock fixtures re-instantiate.

expected.invariant: RecoverOwner pendingRecovery creation is a pure deterministic predicate over witnessed descriptor-chain evidence and witnessed chain facts — no I/O, no clock, no evaluation-time availability callback (ratified SOFTWARE_CANON L2); an implementation that consults a receipt-relative availability callback can fork two honest replayers and is rejected; both pure evaluators converge; identical on deterministic replay
expected.rationale: The core verdict — an evaluation-time availability callback that makes pendingRecovery creation receipt-relative is a conformance failure (the kernel must be a pure deterministic predicate, no I/O/clock, fail-closed) — is derivable from ratified SOFTWARE_CANON ('No DB, no network, no clock' and the boundary rule). This is fail-closed posture on the I/O hole, which is derivable now. But applying it to RecoverOwner pendingRecovery depends on recovery-auth (#50, provisional) to fix WHICH evidence the kernel evaluates (descriptor chain only), and on da-windows (#49) for the §6e clock the predicate reads. So the verdict is derivable but rests on provisional decisions.
